# 📱 Spot-on Prediction for the iPhone 15 Series

Author: Mark View

Date: 2023-08-05

It’s been two months since WWDC23, main actor on WWDC was definitely Vision Pro, it was so bright that it darkened other platforms updates. But if you go through introduction webpage of new OSs, you can still find some updates that might not be very brilliant. Under these maybe boring updates, there’s Swift Data and new SwiftUI features such as animated SF Symbols, phase animation and so on… Then if you toggled the switch of receiving beta updates, and updated to them, you can still find some interesting updates that made Apple devices more future-like.

So I’ll talk about some updates in these beta systems. Check them out!

# Apple Music, Podcasts and System Volume Bar

We might all knew this update that Apple made this control bar floated from the Tab Bar. It’s interesting to see relationships in Apple products. This update is seemingly from Safari on iOS 15. And it made Tab Bar looked gradient. This effect also exists on Search Box in Spotlight. And I think this will be popular throughout Safari and Podcasts in the future. These three apps, all have something above regular Tab Bar or a bottom bar which has some buttons.

Open the Playing page, there’s a smooth transition between the thumbnail of album cover and the big one. You’ll see the control bar extends before the thumbnail is scaled. Then, the thumbnail extends down. During the first several frames, thumbnail and blurry background has clear in-between boundary. Along with the animation, the boundary blends into background with Gradient Blur. Then the dynamic cover will start playing. If you’re listening to a song which has no dynamic cover, the control bar will be blended into colors selected from the cover, and become the background. And you can see more clearly of "former page going back" animation. In the old Apple Music, you can only see the upper part, which made it not so obvious.

But I think this new design will be felt a bit weird in the situation of many albums don’t have dynamic covers. It’s always switching between full screen covers and square ones. I think the major reason Apple doesn’t make stative covers full screen is that covers have many different ratios, not always square. It’s hard to be compatible with covers in different ratios.

Another interesting update is the volume bar icons has bouncing animations, which is a SF Symbols update of this year. And all the volume bars in iOS has this smooth slow-down effect when you rapidly drag them. And new bouncing animation on side volume bar, if you press the physical volume buttons, the volume bar will bounce or shrink slightly.

And Podcasts pages have new layouts, finally modernized!

# Weather app updates, with cool moon phases.

# Nostalgic wallpaper in iPadOS

# AirDrop has a new notification look, which looks quite like Dynamic Island

Before talking about AirDrop notification, let’s talk about notification of Screen Recording. It can be pushed even though Do Not Disturb mode is on. And this is actually a "Dynamic Island feature". And on iPhone 14 Pro, the screen recording notification expands from the Dynamic Island. So strictly speaking, the screen recording "notification", is actually not a notification, but a Dynamic Island activity like Silent Mode switching, low battery noticing. So I think this UI update on AirDrop, is a hint that iPhone 15 will have Dynamic Islands.

There’s another hint, you can find it in SF Symbols 5.0. Let me add some information first. Apple’s system font family is called "SF" aka San Francisco, it includes SF Mono, SF Pro, SF Compact. The latter two each have Display, Text and Rounded font. And SF Symbol is just the symbols you see on the UI, and each symbol has its own name, like this 􀈊 is called 'pencil', adding '.circle' after pencil makes it 􀈋, then adding '.fill' makes it 􀈌. iPhone has its symbols, too. In SF Symbols 5.0, the 'iphone' symbol defaults to the iPhone with Dynamic Island. "iphone.gen1" refers to the iPhone with home buttons, and "iphone.gen2" refers to the iPhone with notch, "iphone.gen3" is the iPhone with Dynamic Island. Since the default iPhone symbols in this version include the Island design, even the iPhone 15, though not as premium as the Pro series, is considered a legitimate member of the iPhone family and will definitely feature the Dynamic Island. History has shown similar examples, such as the mid-end series (iPhone XR), which gained a notch a year after of the release of iPhone X.

Thanks for watching! If you like this video, don’t forget to click like button and subscribe this channel. I’ll post more videos like this in the future.
